title: XOR of numbers in a range

content.problemStatement:
You are given two integers, LLL and RRR. Your task is to calculate the XOR of all numbers in the inclusive range [L,R][L, R][L,R].

Formally, you need to compute:

L⊕(L+1)⊕(L+2)⊕⋯⊕RL \oplus (L+1) \oplus (L+2) \oplus \dots \oplus RL⊕(L+1)⊕(L+2)⊕⋯⊕R

Function DeclarationFunction NamerangeXORrangeXORrangeXOR – This function computes the XOR of all integers in the range [L,R][L, R][L,R].

Parameters
LLL: An integer representing the starting number of the range.
RRR: An integer representing the ending number of the range.
Return Value
Returns an integer representing the XOR of all numbers from LLL to RRR (inclusive).
Constraints
1≤T≤2000001 \leq T \leq 2000001≤T≤200000
1≤L≤R≤1091 \leq L \leq R \leq 10^91≤L≤R≤109

Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases.
Each of the next TTT lines contains two space-separated integers, LLL and RRR.


Output Format

For each test case, print a single line containing the XOR of all integers in the range [L,R][L, R][L,R].
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers L and R (0 ≤ L ≤ R ≤ 10^9).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with the XOR of all integers from L to R inclusive.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each test case we compute XOR of the inclusive range. 2⊕3⊕4⊕5⊕6 = 7, 5⊕6⊕7⊕8⊕9 = 14, 7⊕8⊕9⊕10 = 11.
Only one number, so the XOR equals that number.
Only zero, XOR of a single zero is zero.

solution.approach: The XOR of a consecutive range can be obtained using the property XOR(0..n) = f(n) where f(n) depends only on n mod 4: f(n)=n if n%4==0, f(n)=1 if n%4==1, f(n)=n+1 if n%4==2, f(n)=0 if n%4==3. Then XOR(L..R) = f(R) XOR f(L‑1). For each test case we compute f(R) and f(L‑1) in O(1) time and output their XOR.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.
